content.problemStatement:
Given an integer array ‘A’ of size ‘N’ and an integer ‘K'. Split the array ‘A’ into ‘K’ non-empty subarrays such that the largest sum of any subarray is minimized. Your task is to return the minimized largest sum of the split.
A subarray is a contiguous part of the array.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer 'N' denoting the size of the array 'A'. The second line contains 'N' space-separated integers representing the array 'A'. The third line contains an integer 'K' representing the number of subarrays to split 'A' into.
content.outputFormat: The output will be a single integer representing the minimized largest sum of any subarray after splitting 'A' into 'K' subarrays.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use binary search to find the minimum maximum sum. For each possible maximum sum, count the number of subarrays needed.
